crisis: Displacement from Venezuela to Dominican Republic

tavily.answer: Recent conflicts in Venezuela have led to significant displacement of Venezuelans towards the Dominican Republic. The Dominican Republic has faced security and humanitarian challenges due to the influx. U.S. Deputy Secretary of State Christopher Landau visited the Dominican Republic and Haiti to discuss regional security and economic issues. The earthquakes in Venezuela have caused extensive damage and hampered aid delivery, exacerbating the humanitarian crisis. The Dominican Republic continues to grapple with the impact of these events, including the presence of the Gran Grif gang, which has been responsible for numerous killings.
